events.rationale: Round 1, starting from scratch. My home region is desert (Relics). I have 2 Relics. The blueprint starts with the economic foundation: Shack and Den. The Shack costs 1 Relic and 1 Scrap. I have the Relic, but I lack the Scrap. I cannot build yet. I must gather the missing component. Gathering at the ruins is the only way to get Scrap. I will gather 1 Scrap from the ruins. This sets up the Shack for the next turn. No trades are proposed as the market is empty and I have no surplus to leverage.

Round 1, everyone at 0 VP. I have 2 Fiber (F) from starting hand. Volume wins—need to move resources to generate beads. P1, P2, P3 all hit Ruins, so they likely have Scrap (S) or other non-Fiber resources. I'll propose tiny swaps to P1 and P2 to offload Fiber for Timber (T) or Ore (O), setting up a Shack or Den build next turn. If they bite, I get diversity and beads. If not, I still gather at home to stockpile. Action: Gather at Swamps to maximize Fiber output (2 F) for future trades or buildin

Round 2, early game. Current resources: Rel(2), S(1). Goal is to build Shack (cost: 1 Rel + 1 S) to establish home yield and VP. I have the exact resources needed. P4 offers F for T. I have no T, and F is not needed for the Shack. The trade is mathematically useless for my current blueprint. I will reject the offer and build the Shack immediately to secure the VP and gather bonus.

P4 is offering Fiber for Ore. I have no Ore, so I can't accept. I'm not interested in proposing trades right now; I want to build pressure. I have 2 Timber and 1 Scrap. A Shack costs 1 Timber and 1 Scrap. Building a Shack gives me +1 VP and boosts my home economy. This is the fastest way to get on the board and start generating value. I'll build the Shack.

The table is quiet, but the foundations are being laid. P1 and P2 have secured their Shacks, gaining tempo and home-gather efficiency. P4 is hoarding cards but lacks buildings. I sit at 0 VP with a modest hand of Ore and Scrap. A Shack is the necessary first step to an empire—it grants a VP and boosts my home production, fueling future trades. I have the Ore (2) and Scrap (1) required. Building now keeps me in the race. I will propose a trade to P4, who is resource-rich but building-poor; perhap

Everyone else built Shacks in R2. I'm sitting on 4 Fiber and 0 VP. I need to catch up. P3 wants Timber for Ore. I don't have Timber, but I can flip P3's Ore to P1 or P2 who likely need it for Dens or Forges. I'll propose a swap to P1 (Timber-rich plains) to get the Timber P3 wants, then accept P3's offer to net +1 Ore. That's volume. Then I Gather at home (Swamps) to stack more Fiber for a Shack or Den next turn. Keep the beads moving.

Round 3. I have 1 VP and a Shack. My next logical building is the Den (1 home + 1 non-home + 1S) to boost home yields and reach 2 VP. I currently have 1 Relic. I need 1 Timber, 1 Ore, and 1 Scrap to build. I have no Timber, Ore, or Scrap. P4 is offering 2 Fiber for 1 Timber. I have no Timber to trade, and Fiber is not needed for the Den. Gathering Timber (Plains) or Ore (Mountains) would give me 1 resource, but I need Scrap too. Gathering at Ruins gives Scrap, which is the bottleneck resource fo

P4 is hoarding resources (6 cards) and sitting in last place. They are a fat target, but I have no Scrap to ambush yet. I need to build my engine. I have 1 Timber. Plains is my home, so gathering there gives me 2 Timber (1 base + 1 Shack bonus). That sets me up for a Den next turn or a Forge if I trade smartly. No trades worth proposing right now; I want to hoard my momentum.

The table is quiet—no offers, no beads, just the sound of shuffling feet. P1 and P2 are tied with me at the top, but they're busy gathering basics. P4 is trailing with a fat hand of 6 cards; they're the perfect candidate for a future deal, but I need to grease the wheels first. I'm sitting on a single Ore, which isn't enough to build a Den or a Watchtower. I need to expand my resource palette to attract trade partners. I'll head to the Plains to secure some Timber. It's a safe play, puts a diffe

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 6 Fiber with zero VP and zero partners. I need liquidity and connections. I'll flood the market with Fiber offers to snag Timber and Ore—essential for Den/Watchtower—and Relics for the late-game Great Hall. If I can convert 2 Fiber into 1 Timber and 1 Ore, I'm set for a Den next turn. I'll also try to swap for Relics to keep options open. After pushing these offers, I'll gather at home to keep the Fiber engine running.

I have 1 VP and a Shack. The blueprint requires a Den next to boost home yields, costing 1 Rel, 1 non-home, and 1 Scrap. I currently hold 1 Rel and 1 Scrap. I am missing the non-home resource. P4 is offering 2 Fiber for 1 Timber. I have no Timber, so I cannot accept. I must gather. I need Timber, Ore, or Fiber. Plains (Timber) and Mountains (Ore) are equally viable. I will choose Plains to gather Timber, setting up the Den build next turn.

P4 is trailing with 0 VP and hoarding 8 cards, likely sitting on Fiber. They are desperate for Ore to build. I have 4 Timber and no Ore. I can flip P4's Fiber for Timber, keeping my Timber count high for a Den or Forge while gaining Fiber for a Watchtower or Forge. This 1-for-1 swap fits my tempo style. After the trade, I'll gather at home to fuel my next building push.

P2 wants 1F for 1T. I'm sitting on 8F and zero T. That's a no-brainer—liquidity wins. I take the Timber to open up Den or Watchtower options. With the trade done, I need to keep the engine humming. I'm gathering at home (Swamps) for the 2F yield. I'll be at 9F, 1T, 1Bead. Next turn I can flip that F into more partners or start building. Volume wins.

Current resources: T:1, O:0, F:0, Rel:1, S:1. Goal is to build a Den (cost: 1 Rel + 1 non-home + 1S). I have Rel and S. I am missing the non-home resource (T, O, or F). I have 1 Timber, but I need to keep it to pay the Den cost. Gathering Timber would give me 2 Timber (total 3), which is inefficient as I only need 1. Gathering Ore or Fiber would give me 1 of the required type, satisfying the condition immediately. Mountains (Ore) is the logical choice to secure the missing component. No incoming

I'm tied for the lead with 1 VP, but my economy is fat with 6 Timber and 1 Fiber. I need Ore and Relics to push for a Forge (2 VP) or a Den (1 VP). P1 just hit Mountains, likely taking Ore. I can't ambush without Scrap, and I have no incoming offers to lubricate a swap. I'll hit the Ruins to grab Scrap—this unlocks my ambush capability for future turns and fuels my Forge building costs. It's a low-risk move that sets up my aggression.

The table is quiet, but P4 is hoarding cards in the swamps while P2 scrapes at the ruins. I have a Den ready to lay—costs 1 Ore, 1 Timber, and 1 Scrap. I hold exactly that: 1 Ore, 1 Timber, 1 Scrap. Building now secures the VP and expands my home gathering engine. No need to beg for beads when I can lay brick. I'll propose a trade to P2 just to keep the conversation flowing—offering my future Fiber for their Timber—but I'm building regardless.

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 9 Fiber and 1 Timber. I need Ore and Relics to build a Forge (2 VP) or a Great Hall (4 VP). I'll flood the market with Fiber offers to P2 and P3 to unclog my hand and get the engine moving. P3 just built a Den and might have Ore; P2 is gathering Ruins and might have Relics. I'll gather at Swamps again to keep the pressure on and maximize my home yield.

Current resources: T:1, O:1, F:0, Rel:1, S:1. Target: Den (1 Home + 1 Non-Home + 1S). Home is Desert (Rel). I have Rel and S. Missing Non-Home (T, O, or F). I have T and O. I can build Den immediately using Rel (Home), T (Non-Home), and S. This yields +1 VP (total 2) and +1 Home Gather. This is the optimal economic move to fatten home yields as per the blueprint, avoiding the risk of gathering for a resource I already possess.

P4 is hoarding Fiber (12 cards) and is desperate for Relics. I have 1 Fiber and 0 Relics, but I can leverage P4's greed. I will propose a trade demanding 2 Relics for my 1 Fiber. If P4 accepts, I gain the Relics needed for a Forge or Watchtower. If not, I keep my Fiber. Regardless, I will build a Watchtower. It costs 2 Fiber and 1 Scrap. I have 1 Fiber and 1 Scrap, so I need to gather 1 more Fiber from the swamps. This sets up a Watchtower build next turn to secure my economy against ambushes an

P4 is desperate for Ore to build, offering a premium of 3 Fiber for just 1 Ore. I have no resources, so I must gather first. Mountains is home; with Shack and Den, I yield 3 Ore. I'll trade 1 of those 3 Ore to P4 for the 3 Fiber, securing a new partner and fueling a Forge or Watchtower next turn. The remaining 2 Ore stays in hand. This widens my adjacency and exploits P4's isolation.

Volume wins. P2 wants Relics for Fiber, but I'm empty on Rel. I'll counter-propose a swap to get the Timber I need for a Shack, offering my abundant Fiber. If P2 bites, great; if not, I still Gather at home to keep the engine humming. No time for scouting—cash flow is priority.

I have 2 VP and a solid economic base with Shack and Den. My next logical building is the Forge (+2 VP, +1 gather everywhere), which requires 3 different resource types and Scrap. Currently, I hold 1 Timber and lack Ore, Fiber, Relics, and Scrap. I cannot build yet. I must gather to fill these gaps. My home region (Desert) yields Relics, which I need for the Forge. With Shack and Den, gathering at home yields 4 Relics. This is the most efficient way to acquire a needed resource type. I will gath

P4 is hoarding resources (14 total) and sitting at 0 VP with 0 buildings. They are a fat, slow target. I have 1 Scrap, enough to set an ambush. P4 has been gathering at Swamps (R5, R6) and Plains (R4 implied by trade). I'll ambush the Plains. If P4 goes home to Plains (their likely move to build), I steal 2 Timber. If they go Swamps, I miss, but I force them to scout or play scared. I accept the trade from P4 (3 Fiber for 1 Timber) because it's a 3-for-1 tempo gain that fuels a Den or Watchtower

The plains are teeming with Timber, but P2 has laid an ambush there—a rude interruption waiting to happen. I could scout it, but why play defense when I can play the market? I'm sitting on a mountain of Ore (4 units) with a Den and Shack already built. I don't need more Ore; I need Timber and Fiber to unlock a Forge or a Watchtower. P1 just gathered Relics in the desert, and P4 is hoarding resources in the swamps. I'll propose a trade to P1, offering Ore for their Relics or Fiber—diversifying my

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 10 Fiber and need to convert this inventory into velocity. P2 is the only active trader (partnered R5) and likely needs Fiber for a Den or Forge. I'll float a cheap offer to P2 to move product and secure a bead. If they bite, great; if not, I still execute my primary engine action. I'm gathering at Swamps (home) to net 2 Fiber, pushing my stack to 12. This keeps the pressure on and sets up a potential Forge or Den build next turn while maintaining resource dominance.

I have 2 VP and need to build economic engines. I have 4 Relics and 1 Timber. A Forge (3 different types + Scrap) is the best engine, but I lack Ore, Fiber, and Scrap. A Watchtower (2 of one type + Scrap) is viable if I had Scrap. I have no Scrap. Gathering at home (Desert) yields 2 Relics (Shack+Den bonus), but I already have 4. I need Timber for a Watchtower or Forge. Gathering at Plains yields 1 Timber. I will reject the trade from P3 (2 Ore for 1 Timber + 1 Fiber) because I need my Timber an

P4 is hoarding resources (14 cards) and sitting in last place. They are desperate for Timber to build. I have 5 Timber and 0 Ore. I will bleed them for 3 Ore in exchange for 1 Timber—this lubricates my engine and starves theirs. After that, I'm hitting the Plains. I have a Shack, so I get 3 Timber. This fuels a Forge or Den next turn. P1 just gathered at Plains, but I'm the home tribe; I take the fat yield.

The table is quiet, but P2 is hoarding cards and P4 is trailing—perfect conditions for a deal. I have 4 Ore and need Timber and Fiber to build a Forge, the engine that fuels empires. I'll propose a generous trade to P4, offering 2 Ore for 1 Timber and 1 Fiber. This gives P4 a foothold and me the materials to unlock +1 gather everywhere. If P4 accepts, I'll build the Forge immediately. If not, I'll gather at home to keep the pressure on and stockpile for the next round. No miserly loops here; we

The most practical move is to gather the missing resource I need for the next building. I have no Ore, so gathering at Mountains is the correct load-bearing

I can't build a Den (needs Ore) or Forge (needs Ore/Relics) yet. I need to pivot to a Watchtower (2 Timber + 1 Scrap) to secure my board and grab 2 VP, pushing me closer to the 8 VP win condition. I need to diversify. P2 is hoarding cards (14) and P4 is trailing; both are prime targets for a deal. I'll propose a trade to P2 to swap my abundant Ore for their Timber, aiming to unlock a Watchtower or Forge next turn. If they refuse, I'll still gather Timber to inch closer to a building. Volume wins. I'm sitting on a mountain of Fiber (13) and need to convert it into VP or better assets. I have 1 Bead, so I need 1 more to cash in for +1 VP. I'll propose a tiny trade to P3 (Plains) to get that Bead, offering Fiber which is abundant for me. If that lands, I cash out. Regardless of the trade, I must Gather. Plains (Timber) is the best target to diversify my hand for a future Forge or Den, and P3 just gathered there, so it's likely safe.

With the incoming Ore, I can build a Watchtower (2 Ore + 1 Scrap) for 2 VP, securing my lead and defense. If I don't take the trade, I'm stuck gathering basics.

I'll take it. With the incoming Ore, I'll hit Mountains to grab more Ore and Timber, setting up a build next turn. No need to scout; I'm the wolf, not the sheep.

P4 is trailing and desperate for Scrap to ambush or build, offering a premium of 3 Fiber for just 1 Scrap and 1 Timber. I'm sitting at 5 VP with a fat stack of Fiber (9) and decent Ore (2). I need 1 Ore and 1 Timber to build a Den (+1 VP, +1 Home Gather) and hit 6 VP, keeping the pressure on the leader. P1 just gathered Plains (Timber), so they are a prime target for a quick swap. I'll offer 2 Fiber for 1 Timber. If they bite, I build the Den. If not, I'll still build the Den next turn after a quick Ore grab, but I prefer the tempo now. No ambushes set; I'm building VP.

Round 12, and the table is set for a feast, but the larder is missing a few key ingredients. I sit at 3 VP, comfortably in the middle, but I have my eyes on the Forge—a beautiful engine of industry that grants +2 VP and sweetens every pot I touch. To lay its foundation (3 different types + scrap), I need Fiber and Relics; I have Timber, Ore, and a single Scrap. P2 is the resource baron with 16 cards, likely hoarding the Fiber and Relics I need, while P4 is trailing and desperate for a lifeline.

Trailing bonus is active, so volume wins. P3's offer is a steal: 2T+2O for 1F+1Rel. I have 0 Rel, so I can't accept yet. I need to hit Ruins for Scrap/Relics to unlock that trade and fuel my engine. I'll propose a quick swap to P2 to turn my excess F into O, keeping options open. Then I Gather at Ruins to get the Relic needed to close P3's deal.
